questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Read the following write up carefully and answer the following questions: Consider a parabola, $\mathrm{y}^2=4 \mathrm{ax}$ then $\mathrm{y}+\mathrm{tx}=\mathrm{at}^3+2$ at represents a normal at a point (at ${ }^2, 2 a t$ ). It can also be represented as $y-m x+a m^3+2 a m=0$ in its slope form. Considering the above equation and assuming it passes through a point $P(h, k)$ we can say that $k-m h+a m^3+2 a m=0 \Rightarrow a m^3+m(2 a-h)+k=0$ from $P(h, k)$ maximum 3 normals can be made to the parabola $\mathrm{y}^2=4 a x$Question: Consider a point $P\left(x_1, 0\right)$ lying inside the parabola $y^2=4 a x$ then the interval of $x_1$ such that only one normal passes through $P$ is
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & a m^3+m(2 a-h)+k=0 \\ & \Rightarrow a m^3+m\left(2 a-x_1\right)=0 \\ & \Rightarrow a^2=x_1-2 a\{m=0 \text { (one possible value) }\} \\ & \Rightarrow m^2=\frac{x_1-2 a}{a}, \text { if } x \in(0,2 a) \\ & \Rightarrow m^2=(-) \text { number, so non real roots }\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph:Tangent to the parabola $y=x^2+a x+1$, at the point of intersection of $y$-axis also touches the circle $x^2+y^2=r^2$. Also no point of the parabola is below $x$-axisQuestion:The slope of the tangent when radius of the circle is maximum is
questions.solution: Equation of tangent at $(0,1)$ to the parabola is $y-1=a(x-0)$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Let $S: 4 x^2-4 x-24 y+49=0$ is a parabola and $S_1=0$ represent locus of middle point of chords of parabola $S=0$, which passes through origin. $L_1 \& L_2$ are the tangents for $S=0$ and normals for $x^2+y^2=72$ On the basis of above informations, answer the following questions:Question: Identify the incorrect statement about $S=0-$
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & 4 x^2-4 x+1=24 y-48 \\ & \Rightarrow \quad(2 x-1)^2=24(y-2) \\ & \Rightarrow \quad\left(x-\frac{1}{2}\right)^2=6(y-2)\end{aligned}$
Directrix $y-2=-\frac{3}{2} \quad \Rightarrow y=\frac{1}{2}$ Focus $\left(\frac{1}{2}, 2+\frac{3}{2}\right) \quad \mathrm{LR}=6$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

It can also be represented as $y-m x+a m^3+2 a m=0$ in its slope form. Considering the above equation and assuming it passes through a point $P(h, k)$ we can say that $k-m h+a m^3+2 a m=0 \Rightarrow a m^3+m(2 a-h)+k=0$ from $P(h, k)$ maximum 3 normals can be made to the parabola $\mathrm{y}^2=4 a x$Question: The number of points for the parabola $y^2=4 a x$ from which 3 coincident normals can be drawn is

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Let $S: 4 x^2-4 x-24 y+49=0$ is a parabola and $S_1=0$ represent locus of middle point of chords of parabola $S=0$, which passes through origin. $L_1 \& L_2$ are the tangents for $S=0$ and normals for $x^2+y^2=72$ On the basis of above informations, answer the following questions:Question: $S_1=0$ is -
options: a parabola whose axis is parallel to $y$-axis
a parabola whose axis is parallel to x -axis
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & 4 x^2-4 x+1=24 y-48 \\ & \Rightarrow \quad(2 x-1)^2=24(y-2) \\ & \Rightarrow \quad\left(x-\frac{1}{2}\right)^2=6(y-2)\end{aligned}$
Let mid point is $(h, k)$ so chord is $T=S_1$ $\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow \quad 4 h x-2(x+h)-12(y+k)+49 \\ & =4 h^2-4 h-24 k+49 \end{aligned}$ as it passes through origin it will satisfy $(0,0)$ $\begin{aligned} & \text { so } \quad-2 \mathrm{~h}-12 \mathrm{k}=4 \mathrm{~h}^2-4 \mathrm{~h}-24 \mathrm{k} \\ & \Rightarrow \quad 4 \mathrm{x}^2-2 \mathrm{x}-12 \mathrm{y}=0 \\ & \text { or } \quad 6 \mathrm{y}=2 \mathrm{x}^2-\mathrm{x} \quad \text { which is a parabola. }\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Let $S: 4 x^2-4 x-24 y+49=0$ is a parabola and $S_1=0$ represent locus of middle point of chords of parabola $S=0$, which passes through origin. $L_1 \& L_2$ are the tangents for $S=0$ and normals for $x^2+y^2=72$ On the basis of above informations, answer the following questions:Question: Sum of the slopes of $L_1$ and $L_2$ is -
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & 4 x^2-4 x+1=24 y-48 \\ & \Rightarrow \quad(2 x-1)^2=24(y-2) \\ & \Rightarrow \quad\left(x-\frac{1}{2}\right)^2=6(y-2)\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} & L_1 L_2 \text { must be in the form of } y=m x \\ & \text { solving it with } S=0 \\ & 4 x^2-4 x-24 m x+49=0 \\ & \Rightarrow 4 x^2-4 x(1+6 m)+49=0 \\ & \text { by } D=0 \Rightarrow 16(6 m+1)^2-16.49=0 \\ & 6 \mathrm{~m}+1= \pm 7 \Rightarrow \mathrm{~m}=1 \text { or }-\frac{4}{3} \\ & m_1+m_2=-\frac{1}{3}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Locus of the centroids of equilateral triangle inscribed in the parabola $y^2=9 x$ is a conic section $S=0$. On the basis of above information answer the following :Question: Length of latus rectum of $S=0$ is -
questions.solution:
$\mathrm{y}^2=4 \mathrm{ax}$

Let the centroid is $(\mathrm{h}, \mathrm{k})$ and vertices of $\Delta$ are
$\mathrm{A}\left(\mathrm{at}_1^2, 2 \mathrm{at}_1\right), \mathrm{B}\left(\mathrm{at}_2^2, 2 \mathrm{at}_2\right), \mathrm{C}\left(\mathrm{at}_3^2, 2 \mathrm{at}_3\right)$
So $\quad 3 \mathrm{~h}=\mathrm{a}\left(\mathrm{t}_1^2+\mathrm{t}_2^2+\mathrm{t}_3^2\right)$   ...(i)
$3 \mathrm{k}=2 \mathrm{a}\left(\mathrm{t}_1+\mathrm{t}_2+\mathrm{t}_3\right)$   ...(ii)
Let $r$ is the radius of circle and $t_1, t_2, t_3$ are roots of equation $\left(a t^2-h\right)^2+(2 a t-k)^2=r^2$
$\Rightarrow a^2 t^4+t^2\left(4 a^2-2 a h\right)-4 a k t+h^2+k^2-r^2=0$
$t_1+t_2+t_3+t_4=0, \Sigma t_1 t_2=\frac{4 a^2-2 a h}{a^2}$
$\begin{aligned}
& \text { by (ii) } t_4=-\left(\frac{3 \mathrm{k}}{2 \mathrm{a}}\right) \\
& \text { by }\left(\mathrm{t}_1+\mathrm{t}_2+\mathrm{t}_3+\mathrm{t}_4\right)^2=0 \\
& \Rightarrow \quad \Sigma \mathrm{t}_1^2=-2 \Sigma \mathrm{t}_1 \mathrm{t}_2 \\
& \frac{3 \mathrm{~h}}{\mathrm{a}}+\left(\frac{9 \mathrm{k}^2}{4 \mathrm{a}^2}\right)=-2\left(\frac{4 \mathrm{a}^2-2 \mathrm{ah}}{\mathrm{a}^2}\right) \\
& \Rightarrow \quad 3 \mathrm{ah}+\frac{9 \mathrm{k}^2}{4}=-8 \mathrm{a}^2+4 \mathrm{ah} \\
& \Rightarrow \quad 9 \mathrm{k}^2=-32 \mathrm{a}^2+4 \mathrm{ah} \Rightarrow 9 \mathrm{y}^2=4 \mathrm{a}(\mathrm{x}-8 \mathrm{a})
\end{aligned}$
here $\mathrm{a}=\frac{9}{4}$ so locus is $\mathrm{y}^2=(\mathrm{x}-18)$

$\text { So } \mathrm{LR}=1$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: Paragraph: Locus of the centroids of equilateral triangle inscribed in the parabola $y^2=9 x$ is a conic section $S=0$. On the basis of above information answer the following :Question: Locus of point of intersection of perpendicular tangents of $S=0$ is -
questions.solution:
$\mathrm{y}^2=4 \mathrm{ax}$

Let the centroid is $(\mathrm{h}, \mathrm{k})$ and vertices of $\Delta$ are
$\mathrm{A}\left(\mathrm{at}_1^2, 2 \mathrm{at}_1\right), \mathrm{B}\left(\mathrm{at}_2^2, 2 \mathrm{at}_2\right), \mathrm{C}\left(\mathrm{at}_3^2, 2 \mathrm{at}_3\right)$
So $\quad 3 \mathrm{~h}=\mathrm{a}\left(\mathrm{t}_1^2+\mathrm{t}_2^2+\mathrm{t}_3^2\right)$   ...(i)
$3 \mathrm{k}=2 \mathrm{a}\left(\mathrm{t}_1+\mathrm{t}_2+\mathrm{t}_3\right)$   ...(ii)
Let $r$ is the radius of circle and $t_1, t_2, t_3$ are roots of equation $\left(a t^2-h\right)^2+(2 a t-k)^2=r^2$
$\Rightarrow a^2 t^4+t^2\left(4 a^2-2 a h\right)-4 a k t+h^2+k^2-r^2=0$
$t_1+t_2+t_3+t_4=0, \Sigma t_1 t_2=\frac{4 a^2-2 a h}{a^2}$
$\begin{aligned}
& \text { by (ii) } t_4=-\left(\frac{3 \mathrm{k}}{2 \mathrm{a}}\right) \\
& \text { by }\left(\mathrm{t}_1+\mathrm{t}_2+\mathrm{t}_3+\mathrm{t}_4\right)^2=0 \\
& \Rightarrow \quad \Sigma \mathrm{t}_1^2=-2 \Sigma \mathrm{t}_1 \mathrm{t}_2 \\
& \frac{3 \mathrm{~h}}{\mathrm{a}}+\left(\frac{9 \mathrm{k}^2}{4 \mathrm{a}^2}\right)=-2\left(\frac{4 \mathrm{a}^2-2 \mathrm{ah}}{\mathrm{a}^2}\right) \\
& \Rightarrow \quad 3 \mathrm{ah}+\frac{9 \mathrm{k}^2}{4}=-8 \mathrm{a}^2+4 \mathrm{ah} \\
& \Rightarrow \quad 9 \mathrm{k}^2=-32 \mathrm{a}^2+4 \mathrm{ah} \Rightarrow 9 \mathrm{y}^2=4 \mathrm{a}(\mathrm{x}-8 \mathrm{a})
\end{aligned}$
here $\mathrm{a}=\frac{9}{4}$ so locus is $\mathrm{y}^2=(\mathrm{x}-18)$

Locus of point of intersection of perpendicular tangents is directrix $\mathrm{x}-18=-\frac{1}{4}$ $\Rightarrow \quad 4 x-71=0$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: Paragraph: Locus of the centroids of equilateral triangle inscribed in the parabola $y^2=9 x$ is a conic section $S=0$. On the basis of above information answer the following :Question: Chord of contact of pair of tangents drawn from origin to $S=0$ meet $x$-axis at $(\alpha, 0)$, then $\alpha$ is -
questions.solution:
$\mathrm{y}^2=4 \mathrm{ax}$

Let the centroid is $(\mathrm{h}, \mathrm{k})$ and vertices of $\Delta$ are
$\mathrm{A}\left(\mathrm{at}_1^2, 2 \mathrm{at}_1\right), \mathrm{B}\left(\mathrm{at}_2^2, 2 \mathrm{at}_2\right), \mathrm{C}\left(\mathrm{at}_3^2, 2 \mathrm{at}_3\right)$
So $\quad 3 \mathrm{~h}=\mathrm{a}\left(\mathrm{t}_1^2+\mathrm{t}_2^2+\mathrm{t}_3^2\right)$   ...(i)
$3 \mathrm{k}=2 \mathrm{a}\left(\mathrm{t}_1+\mathrm{t}_2+\mathrm{t}_3\right)$   ...(ii)
Let $r$ is the radius of circle and $t_1, t_2, t_3$ are roots of equation $\left(a t^2-h\right)^2+(2 a t-k)^2=r^2$
$\Rightarrow a^2 t^4+t^2\left(4 a^2-2 a h\right)-4 a k t+h^2+k^2-r^2=0$
$t_1+t_2+t_3+t_4=0, \Sigma t_1 t_2=\frac{4 a^2-2 a h}{a^2}$
$\begin{aligned}
& \text { by (ii) } t_4=-\left(\frac{3 \mathrm{k}}{2 \mathrm{a}}\right) \\
& \text { by }\left(\mathrm{t}_1+\mathrm{t}_2+\mathrm{t}_3+\mathrm{t}_4\right)^2=0 \\
& \Rightarrow \quad \Sigma \mathrm{t}_1^2=-2 \Sigma \mathrm{t}_1 \mathrm{t}_2 \\
& \frac{3 \mathrm{~h}}{\mathrm{a}}+\left(\frac{9 \mathrm{k}^2}{4 \mathrm{a}^2}\right)=-2\left(\frac{4 \mathrm{a}^2-2 \mathrm{ah}}{\mathrm{a}^2}\right) \\
& \Rightarrow \quad 3 \mathrm{ah}+\frac{9 \mathrm{k}^2}{4}=-8 \mathrm{a}^2+4 \mathrm{ah} \\
& \Rightarrow \quad 9 \mathrm{k}^2=-32 \mathrm{a}^2+4 \mathrm{ah} \Rightarrow 9 \mathrm{y}^2=4 \mathrm{a}(\mathrm{x}-8 \mathrm{a})
\end{aligned}$
here $\mathrm{a}=\frac{9}{4}$ so locus is $\mathrm{y}^2=(\mathrm{x}-18)$

$\begin{aligned}
&\text { Chord of contact is } \mathrm{T}=0\\
&\begin{aligned}
& 0 . y=\frac{x+0}{2}-18 \\
& x=36, \text { so }(36,0)
\end{aligned}
\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: A variable circle passes through the point $A(2,1)$ and touches the $x$-axis. Locus of the other end of the diameter through A is a parabola.Question: The length of the latus rectum of the parabola, is
questions.solution:
(i) Equation of the variable circles $\begin{gathered} (x-h)(x-2)+(y-k)(y-1)=0 \\ x^2+y^2-(2+h) x-(k+1) y+k+2 h=0 \end{gathered}$
As x -intercept $=0$ $\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow \mathrm{g}^2=\mathrm{c} \\ & \therefore \quad \frac{(\mathrm{~h}+2)^2}{4}=\mathrm{k}+2 \mathrm{~h} \\ & \Rightarrow \quad(\mathrm{~h}+2)^2=4 \mathrm{k}+8 \mathrm{~h} \\ & \Rightarrow(\mathrm{~h}-2)^2=4 \mathrm{k} \end{aligned}$ $\therefore \quad$ Locus is $(x-2)^2=4 y \Rightarrow$ L.R. $=4$ Ans. (ii) $\mathrm{N} \equiv(2,-1)$ Ans.
(iii) Figure is a square. $\therefore \quad$ Area $=\frac{(4)(4)}{2}=8$ sq. units Ans.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.q: Paragraph: A variable circle passes through the point $A(2,1)$ and touches the $x$-axis. Locus of the other end of the diameter through A is a parabola.Question: The coordinates of the foot of the directrix of the parabola, is
questions.solution:
(i) Equation of the variable circles $\begin{gathered} (x-h)(x-2)+(y-k)(y-1)=0 \\ x^2+y^2-(2+h) x-(k+1) y+k+2 h=0 \end{gathered}$
As x -intercept $=0$ $\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow \mathrm{g}^2=\mathrm{c} \\ & \therefore \quad \frac{(\mathrm{~h}+2)^2}{4}=\mathrm{k}+2 \mathrm{~h} \\ & \Rightarrow \quad(\mathrm{~h}+2)^2=4 \mathrm{k}+8 \mathrm{~h} \\ & \Rightarrow(\mathrm{~h}-2)^2=4 \mathrm{k} \end{aligned}$ $\therefore \quad$ Locus is $(x-2)^2=4 y \Rightarrow$ L.R. $=4$ Ans. (ii) $\mathrm{N} \equiv(2,-1)$ Ans.
(iii) Figure is a square. $\therefore \quad$ Area $=\frac{(4)(4)}{2}=8$ sq. units Ans.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
